clinical_history: This ECG from a middle-aged man shows which ONE of the following arrhythmias?
diagnosis: The ECG shows atrial fibrillation (AF) with a very rapid  ventricular response (about 168/min at rest here). Artifactual drift of the baseline is present. There are nonspecific ST-T changes and relatively slow R wave progression, also a non-diagnostic finding.  The patient had a history of systemic hypertension. Echocardiogram showed mild concentric left ventricular hypertrophy (not evident on this ECG) and normal overall ventricular and valvular function.
